title: Interpret the Response From an Initialize Tote Request
summary: Use the Response field on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page to determine whether tote initialization succeeded or why it was rejected. The source states that successful requests show confirmation, while unsuccessful requests show an error message or tote status. The source also notes an AGV-related limitation where the response may indicate the tote cannot be initialized.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing the result of an Initialize Tote request on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page and you need to determine whether the tote was initialized successfully or identify the source-reported reason it was not initialized.
not_for: Not for correcting or resolving initialization failures when the source only provides the returned response meaning and not a corrective workflow.
Not for diagnosing issues outside the Response field behavior described in the source.
Not for performing tote initialization from another interface not identified as the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the Initialize Tote Aveva API page Response field and interpret whether the request succeeded, returned an error, or reported tote status.

safety_notes: This source describes interpretation of a response field only; it does not provide corrective actions for failed initialization responses.
If the response indicates the tote cannot be initialized and no corrective action is provided in the source, escalate rather than inventing a recovery action.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Initialize Tote Aveva API page
Visibility of the Response field after a request
Source-backed understanding of confirmation versus error/status responses

access_required: Access to the Initialize Tote Aveva API page
system_preconditions: An Initialize Tote request has been submitted or is available for review on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.
The Response field is visible after the request.

visual_references.description: Initialize Tote Aveva API page used to submit a tote initialization request and review the Response field result.

steps.title: Open or review the Initialize Tote request
steps.purpose: Ensure the request context being interpreted is the Initialize Tote request on the correct page.
steps.instruction: Submit or review an Initialize Tote request on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page before interpreting the returned result.
steps.expected_result: The Initialize Tote request context is available for review on the page.
steps.healthy_condition: The Initialize Tote Aveva API page is visible and the request has been submitted or is available for review.
steps.failure_condition: The request is not available for review or the user is not on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page described by the source.

actions.label: Review initialize tote request
actions.exact_action: Submit or review an Initialize Tote request on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: The page being reviewed is not the Initialize Tote Aveva API page supported by the source.

steps.title: Locate the Response field
steps.purpose: Identify the field that displays the result of the Initialize Tote request.
steps.instruction: Locate the Response field on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.
steps.expected_result: The Response field is identified on the page.
steps.healthy_condition: The Response field is visible and can be read.
steps.failure_condition: The Response field cannot be located or is not visible after the request.

actions.label: Find response field
actions.exact_action: Locate the Response field on the page.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Response field is not visible or cannot be identified on the page.

Artifact shows the Initialize Tote Aveva API page referenced by the training segment.

steps.title: Check for a confirmation message
steps.instruction: Check whether the Response field shows a confirmation message, which indicates the initialization was successful.
steps.expected_result: You can determine whether the request succeeded based on the presence of a confirmation message.
steps.healthy_condition: The Response field shows confirmation indicating the tote was initialized successfully.
steps.failure_condition: The Response field does not show confirmation and instead returns an error message or tote status.

actions.label: Review confirmation status
actions.exact_action: Check whether the Response field shows a confirmation message, which indicates the initialization was successful.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Response field result area used to confirm whether initialization succeeded.

stop_or_escalate_if: The response behavior does not align with the source statement that successful requests show confirmation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Response field displays the result of initialization; successful requests show confirmation and failed requests show an error message or tote status.

steps.title: Read the returned error or tote status
steps.purpose: Identify the source-reported reason the tote was not initialized.
steps.instruction: If the request did not succeed, read the returned error message or tote status shown in the Response field.
steps.expected_result: The returned error message or tote status is identified from the Response field.
steps.healthy_condition: The response text clearly indicates the error or tote status associated with the failed initialization.
steps.failure_condition: The response text is missing, unclear, or does not fit the source-described behavior of error message or tote status on failure.

actions.label: Read failure response
actions.exact_action: If the request did not succeed, read the returned error message or tote status shown in the Response field.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Response field where error information or tote status is displayed when initialization does not succeed.

stop_or_escalate_if: The response does not match the documented confirmation, error, or tote status behavior described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Response field displays the result of initialization; successful requests show confirmation and failed requests show an error message or tote status.

steps.title: Compare the message to documented examples
steps.purpose: Interpret the returned message using the examples explicitly described in the source.
steps.instruction: Compare the returned message to the source-backed examples, including the note that a tote on an AGV may return a response saying it cannot be initialized.
steps.expected_result: The returned message is interpreted against the source examples, including the AGV-related limitation.
steps.healthy_condition: The response aligns with the documented examples such as confirmation, error, tote status, or an AGV-related cannot-initialize message.
steps.failure_condition: The response indicates the tote cannot be initialized or otherwise does not match documented examples and the source provides no corrective action.

actions.label: Compare to source examples
actions.exact_action: Compare the returned message to the source-backed examples, including the note that a tote on an AGV may return a response saying it cannot be initialized.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Initialize Tote page context referenced by the training segment while discussing response meanings and AGV-related limitations.

stop_or_escalate_if: The response indicates the tote cannot be initialized and the source does not provide a corrective action.
The response does not match the documented confirmation, error, or tote status behavior described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript says that if totes are on an AGV, the response may say the tote cannot be initialized, with references to AGVs not in the system or removed AGVs.
Training segment describes AGV-related constraints for Initialize Tote, including responses that a tote cannot be initialized when associated with an AGV.

steps.title: Record the response text exactly
steps.purpose: Preserve the exact returned status or error for follow-up and escalation.
steps.instruction: Record the returned status or error text exactly as shown if the initialization did not complete.
steps.expected_result: The exact response text is captured for reference or escalation.
steps.healthy_condition: The returned status or error text is documented exactly as displayed in the Response field.
steps.failure_condition: The response text is paraphrased, incomplete, or not captured when the initialization did not complete.

actions.label: Document exact response
actions.exact_action: Record the returned status or error text exactly as shown if the initialization did not complete.

stop_or_escalate_if: The response cannot be captured exactly.
The response indicates the tote cannot be initialized and no corrective action is provided in the source.

success_criteria: The user can determine from the Response field whether the tote was initialized successfully.
If initialization did not succeed, the user can identify the source-reported error message or tote status.
If initialization did not complete, the returned status or error text is recorded exactly as shown.
healthy_conditions: The Response field is visible on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.
A confirmation message is shown when initialization succeeds.
Returned messages align with the source-described behavior of confirmation, error message, or tote status.
failure_conditions: The Response field cannot be located or read.
The response does not match the documented confirmation, error, or tote status behavior described in the source.
The response indicates the tote cannot be initialized and the source provides no corrective action.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the response does not match the documented confirmation, error, or tote status behavior described in the source.
Escalate if the response indicates the tote cannot be initialized and the source does not provide a corrective action.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact confirmation message text.
The source does not provide exact error message text beyond the described behaviors and AGV-related example.
The source does not provide a corrective workflow for AGV-related cannot-initialize responses.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify production stop or LOTO requirements.
